message:
feat(layout-lab,wasm): add interactive pane studio with drag/dock/timeline
and engine-backed selection copy

Transform the Layout Lab from a static constraint visualizer into a full
pane management workspace: drag-to-reflow, edge-grip resize, Shift+click
multi-select, docking ghost overlays with zone labels, undo/redo/replay
timeline, four intelligence modes (Focus/Compare/Monitor/Compact),
inertial throw physics, and magnetic field snapping. Update handle_mouse
to accept modifier keys throughout.

In frankenterm-web, upgrade selection text extraction to prefer the core
engine's Selection::extract_copy() over the shadow-cell fallback, fixing
combining mark and row-boundary newline correctness. The shadow-cell path
remains as a fallback when no engine is attached.

Rewire the dashboard TextFx panel to navigate to Layout Lab instead of
Visual Effects. Sync beads issue tracker timestamps.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

 {"id":"bd-2vr05.4","title":"feature(frankenterm-js): selection, copy, and clipboard parity","description":"Background
- Selection and clipboard behavior are non-negotiable for terminal workflows (copy logs, commands, diagnostics).
- Edge cases around wraps, unicode, and scrolling are where many terminal emulators regress.

Goal
- Deliver resilient selection/copy/clipboard behavior with explicit policy and strong test coverage.

In Scope
- Rust-first selection state machine.
- Mouse and keyboard selection gestures.
- Extraction semantics for wrapped and unicode-rich content.
- Clipboard integration and paste-safety interactions.
- Stress testing under churn (output, scroll, resize).

Out of Scope
- Browser-specific one-off hacks that bypass core state model.

Why This Matters
- Reliable selection is a key adoption blocker for replacing xterm.js.

Acceptance Criteria
- Selection remains correct under heavy output and viewport changes.
- Copy output matches documented policy for wraps and graphemes.
- Clipboard flows are deterministic and test-backed.","acceptance_criteria":"## Acceptance Criteria
- Feature behavior is validated by comprehensive unit tests that cover core invariants and edge cases.
- Feature behavior is validated by end-to-end test scripts that exercise realistic user workflows.
- E2E runs emit detailed structured logs (JSONL or equivalent) sufficient for deterministic failure triage and replay.
- CI gates for this feature fail on regression in correctness, determinism, or declared standards scope.","status":"in_progress","priority":1,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-02-11T02:48:34.723118448Z","created_by":"HazyFalcon","updated_at":"2026-02-15T02:18:32.592414950Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["clipboard","frankenterm","selection","ux"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-2vr05.4","depends_on_id":"bd-2vr05","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-11T02:48:34.723118448Z","created_by":"HazyFalcon","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-2vr05.4","depends_on_id":"bd-2vr05.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-11T02:49:03.985157787Z","created_by":"HazyFalcon","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}

 {"id":"bd-2vr05.5","title":"feature(frankenterm-js): terminal serialization and export APIs (ANSI/HTML/text)","description":"Background
- Export APIs are essential for logs, support artifacts, snapshots, and migration workflows.
- Mature terminals expose practical serialization options beyond on-screen rendering.

Goal
- Provide deterministic and documented serialization APIs for ANSI, HTML, and plain text.

In Scope
- API contract and options schema.
- ANSI serializer with style fidelity.
- HTML serializer with safe escaping and hyperlink fidelity.
- Plain-text serializer with clear wrap/space policy.
- Golden test matrix to prevent drift.

Out of Scope
- Ad hoc export behavior without explicit option contracts.

Why This Matters
- Serialization enables ecosystem workflows and strengthens debug/test ergonomics.

Acceptance Criteria
- Export outputs are deterministic for fixed input snapshots.
- Golden fixtures cover representative styling/unicode cases.
- API semantics are documented and versioned.","acceptance_criteria":"## Acceptance Criteria
- Feature behavior is validated by comprehensive unit tests that cover core invariants and edge cases.
- Feature behavior is validated by end-to-end test scripts that exercise realistic user workflows.
- E2E runs emit detailed structured logs (JSONL or equivalent) sufficient for deterministic failure triage and replay.
- CI gates for this feature fail on regression in correctness, determinism, or declared standards scope.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-02-11T02:48:35.145281825Z","created_by":"HazyFalcon","updated_at":"2026-02-11T03:03:15.418489448Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["api","export","frankenterm","serialization"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-2vr05.5","depends_on_id":"bd-2vr05","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-11T02:48:35.145281825Z","created_by":"HazyFalcon","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-2vr05.5","depends_on_id":"bd-2vr05.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-11T02:49:04.363130491Z","created_by":"HazyFalcon","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}

 {"id":"bd-fbp","title":"Implement inline mode overlay redraw baseline","description":"PHASE 2.5 - INLINE MODE IMPLEMENTATION (CRITICAL)

## Background
Inline mode is a NON-NEGOTIABLE requirement. The overlay redraw baseline is the correctness foundation that works everywhere, before optimization.

## Technical Requirements (from Plan Section D)

### Overlay Redraw Strategy
1. Save cursor position (DECSC or manual tracking)
2. Move cursor to UI region start
3. Clear UI region only (never full screen!)
4. Render UI frame
5. Restore cursor position (DECRC)

### UI Region Anchoring
- Bottom-anchored (default): UI at bottom, logs scroll above
- Top-anchored (optional): UI at top, logs scroll below
- Configurable height: ui_height parameter

### Log Write Coordination
write_log() must:
1. Suspend UI temporarily
2. Print log line to scrollback region
3. Re-render UI in its anchored position
4. Maintain cursor contract

### Cursor Contract
After any present():
- Cursor is at the correct position per active policy
- UI region is intact
- Scrollback is preserved

### Never Clear Full Screen
The DECRC/ED2 pattern that clears full screen is FORBIDDEN in inline mode.
Only clear the bounded UI region using EL (erase line) or localized ED.

## Implementation

pub struct InlinePresenter {
    ui_height: u16,
    anchor: Anchor,  // Bottom or Top
    cursor_saved: bool,
}

impl InlinePresenter {
    pub fn present(&mut self, buffer: &Buffer, writer: &mut TerminalWriter) {
        // 1. Save cursor
        writer.write_all(b"\x1b7")?;  // DECSC
        
        // 2. Move to UI region
        let (_, term_height) = writer.size();
        let ui_start = match self.anchor {
            Anchor::Bottom => term_height - self.ui_height,
            Anchor::Top => 0,
        };
        writer.move_to(0, ui_start)?;
        
        // 3. Clear UI region only (line by line)
        for row in 0..self.ui_height {
            writer.write_all(b"\x1b[2K")?;  // EL - clear line
            if row < self.ui_height - 1 {
                writer.write_all(b"\n")?;
            }
        }
        
        // 4. Move back and render
        writer.move_to(0, ui_start)?;
        // ... emit diff ...
        
        // 5. Restore cursor
        writer.write_all(b"\x1b8")?;  // DECRC
    }
}

## Tests Required
- [ ] UI renders at correct position (bottom/top)
- [ ] Scrollback is preserved after present()
- [ ] Cursor returns to correct position
- [ ] Log writes don't corrupt UI
- [ ] Resize re-anchors correctly
- [ ] PTY test: verify no scrollback corruption

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Overlay redraw works on all terminals
- [ ] Cursor contract maintained
- [ ] UI region bounded, never full-screen clear
- [ ] Log coordination implemented
- [ ] PTY tests pass

## Performance Budget
- Overlay redraw: < 2ms (acceptable for correctness baseline)

 {"id":"bd-fp38v","title":"epic(alien-artifact): Unified Evidence Ledger Schema Across All Decision Points","description":"Unify ALL Bayesian decision points (diff strategy, resize coalescing, frame budget, degradation, VOI, hint ranking, command palette scoring) under a single structured Evidence Ledger schema. Every decision emits: log_posterior, top-3 evidence terms with Bayes factors, action chosen, loss avoided, confidence interval. EV=10.0 (cross-project). Enables galaxy-brain transparency, postmortems, and guardrail automation.

**User Value:** Every Bayesian decision is fully auditable via structured evidence logs, enabling developers to understand and debug adaptive behavior.

**Acceptance Criteria:**
- Unified EvidenceLedger struct covers all 7 decision domains
- JSONL output with decision_id, evidence_terms, posterior, action
- All Bayesian controllers emit evidence on every decision
- Evidence replay produces identical decisions
- All existing tests pass","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2026-02-13T21:50:11.520508236Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-14T09:18:17.608200473Z","closed_at":"2026-02-14T09:18:17.608136213Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["alien-artifact","evidence","observability"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-fp38v","depends_on_id":"bd-nv2fa","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-13T21:50:34.895615197Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"bd-fp38v.1","title":"task(e2e): E2E test suite for Evidence Ledger","description":"## E2E Integration Test: Evidence Ledger (EV=10)

### Scope
Full integration test rendering with evidence emission enabled across all 7 Bayesian decision points. Verifies schema compliance, no missing fields, and correct evidence structure for every decision.

### Test Scenario
1. Enable evidence ledger emission for all 7 decision points:
   a. Diff strategy selection (incremental vs full)
   b. Resize coalescing (coalesce vs immediate)
   c. Frame budget management (admit vs defer)
   d. Degradation level selection (normal/reduced/minimal)
   e. VOI (value of information) for measurement decisions
   f. Hint ranking for command palette
   g. Command palette scoring
2. Render demo screens that exercise each decision point.
3. For diff strategy: alternate between small edits (incremental) and full redraws.
4. For resize: send rapid resize events.
5. For frame budget: inject artificial load spikes.
6. For degradation: gradually increase frame times.
7. Collect all JSONL evidence entries and validate schema.

### JSONL Structured Logging Specification
Each decision point emits a unified evidence entry:
```json
{
  "event": "evidence_ledger",
  "ts_ns": u64,
  "decision_point": "diff_strategy|resize_coalesce|frame_budget|degradation|voi|hint_rank|cmd_score",
  "log_posterior": f64,
  "top_evidence": [
    { "term": "string", "bayes_factor": f64, "direction": "for|against" }
  ],
  "action_chosen": "string",
  "loss_avoided": f64,
  "confidence_interval": { "lower": f64, "upper": f64, "level": f64 },
  "alternatives_considered": u32,
  "frame_id": u64
}
```

### Success Criteria
- All 7 decision points emit at least one evidence entry during the test run.
- Every evidence entry has all required fields (no nulls in required fields).
- top_evidence array has exactly 3 entries per decision (top-3 evidence terms).
- bayes_factor values are positive and finite (no NaN/Inf).
- confidence_interval.lower < confidence_interval.upper.
- Schema validates against JSON Schema definition.
- No panics, no unsafe code (#![forbid(unsafe_code)]).
- JSONL log is parseable and every line deserializes without error.","status":"in_progress","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-13T22:25:34.466502948Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-14T05:07:35.760241543Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["e2e","logging","test"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-fp38v.1","depends_on_id":"bd-6i8zb","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-13T22:27:07.857688982Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-fp38v.1","depends_on_id":"bd-fp38v","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-13T22:25:34.466502948Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"bd-fq968","title":"Mermaid init directives: snapshots + E2E JSONL logging","description":"Goal: once TME rendering exists, add snapshot coverage for init directives (theme + direction) and E2E JSONL logs including init_config_hash + warnings.\n\nScope\n- Add render snapshots that demonstrate themeVariables + flowchart.direction effects on diagrams.\n- Add PTY E2E flow covering init directives; JSONL should include init_config_hash and warnings.\n- Ensure deterministic seeds and stable hashes.\n\nNotes\n- Blocked until Mermaid TME renderer + terminal renderer is implemented (bd-hudcn/bd-12o4t).","notes":"Prep plan (blocked on bd-hudcn/bd-12o4t):
- Snapshot cases: flowchart w/ init {theme: 'dark' or 'base', themeVariables:{primaryColor,lineColor}} + flowchart.direction (TB/LR) to verify direction + theme overrides.
- Sizes: 80x24 + 120x40; deterministic seed/time via demo determinism fixtures (ftui-demo-showcase/src/determinism.rs).
- E2E: extend demo sweep script to run mermaid fixture with init directives; JSONL fields include init_config_hash, init_theme, init_theme_vars count, init_warnings[], flow_direction, size, mode, checksum.
- Failure mode: if renderer not available, test should skip with clear message (avoid compatibility shims in code path).

Recon:
- Mermaid init directive fixture: crates/ftui-extras/tests/fixtures/mermaid/graph_init_directive.mmd.
- Mermaid JSONL events already emitted: mermaid_prepare includes init_config_hash (crates/ftui-extras/src/mermaid.rs).
- Demo sweep script: tests/e2e/scripts/test_demo_showcase_sweep.sh (JSONL helpers in tests/e2e/lib/logging.sh).

Progress (2026-02-05):
- Added JSONL tests for mermaid_prepare + mermaid_guard in crates/ftui-extras/src/mermaid.rs (temp log helper; no file deletion).
- Fixed layout wiring: compute_node_sizes + pass cluster_map into minimize_crossings; allow clippy::too_many_arguments on multi_rank_waypoints in crates/ftui-extras/src/mermaid_layout.rs.
- Quality gates: cargo fmt --check, cargo check --all-targets, cargo clippy --all-targets -- -D warnings (green).","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"GreenDesert","created_at":"2026-02-05T05:23:39.515062209Z","created_by":"GoldWaterfall","updated_at":"2026-02-08T23:03:48.223899436Z","closed_at":"2026-02-08T23:03:48.223879509Z","close_reason":"Completed: init-directive snapshots + PTY/E2E mermaid_prepare JSONL (init hash + warnings)","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["e2e","mermaid","testing"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-fq968","depends_on_id":"bd-12o4t","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-05T05:23:39.515062209Z","created_by":"GoldWaterfall","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-fq968","depends_on_id":"bd-hudcn","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-05T05:23:39.515062209Z","created_by":"GoldWaterfall","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":998,"issue_id":"bd-fq968","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Starting bd-fq968: will add targeted init-directive snapshots (themeVariables + direction) and extend PTY/E2E JSONL assertions to include init_config_hash + warnings for init-directive samples.","created_at":"2026-02-07T21:15:44Z"}]}
